user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator runs sworn capabilities or sworn models and sees truthful per-prefix reachability for keyless local servers, then runs sworn run with a local implementer model and the implementer leg reaches that server while capable configured models complete the structured roles.
rationale: S01 makes endpoints constructible; this slice makes them reachable through the loop and honest in enumeration. Local prefixes cannot share keyProbe because they have no key, and a preflight before each dispatch would be redundant and stale. Enumeration therefore uses a bounded models-list probe while dispatch relies on the real request. The local registry entry continues to declare all in-process roles so S04/S05 can later admit each model by capability rather than a provider-name allowlist. This slice owns contract C-03 and consumes C-01/C-04.
in_scope: Register ollama-cloud/ with the credentialed OAI chat identity and register ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/ in a separate keyless OAI chat entry whose stable identity is oai-local-inprocess; derive both prefix groups from S01 endpoint descriptors rather than another literal prefix list.

The keyless local in-process identity declares implementer, verifier, and captain roles exactly like the shared OAI chat driver. This slice does not claim every local model has the structured capability needed for every role; S04/S05 performs that later model-level admission.

Implement a local reachability probe that performs GET <base_url>/models with a two-second total request timeout, no retries, no Authorization header, and availability only for HTTP 2xx plus a parseable OpenAI models object with a data array.

Run local endpoint probes concurrently during sworn capabilities so five unreachable defaults cost approximately one timeout window rather than five serial windows. Render each canonical prefix as reachable or unavailable with a secret/payload-safe reason; the command remains an enumeration and does not send chat completions.

Make sworn models use each configured local OAI /models endpoint and returned IDs instead of native Ollama /api/tags for ollama/; ollama-native/ retains the existing native catalog semantics if exposed by the command.

Thread the config-derived model.ProviderConfig/registry from cmd/sworn into capabilities, models, single-task run, release run, parallel startup resolution, and every RunSlice call. Production command paths must not fall back to a config-blind ProviderConfigFromEnv after config.Load has succeeded.

Add a command-level end-to-end fixture in which sworn run uses ollama/<model> for the implementer and capable configured fixture models for captain/verifier, exercises real HTTP transport plus the agent tool loop in a temporary git workspace, and reaches the verified/merge outcome with the implementer endpoint request recorded.

Add an opt-in live local-Ollama reachability path driven by an explicitly supplied local model ID. Record the real sworn run output/exit code in proof; if no suitable local daemon/model is available, record the exact skip reason rather than claiming coverage.

Produce the required mutation proof: route a local descriptor through keyProbe and observe unavailable with no key, then restore the reachability probe against a live httptest transport and observe available. Preserve both outputs in proof.json/journal.
out_of_scope: Endpoint descriptor construction, config schema, credential mapping, and ollama/ prefix migration are S01-endpoint-registry-config.
Endpoint dialect generation and runtime request shaping are S03-endpoint-dialect-conformance.
Nightly provider matrix orchestration is S04-live-provider-matrix.
Making a capability-insufficient local model serve captain/verifier is forbidden; S04/S05 owns capability discovery and eligibility, and sworn#86 owns subprocess captain support.
Caching availability or preflighting before each dispatch is excluded; the real dispatch request remains authoritative.

effort_complexity.rationale: This is a broad integration slice spanning registry identity, concurrent network probes, catalog behaviour, config propagation, and the full run command. Correctness depends on keeping advertised availability, role resolution, and actual dispatch aligned across single and parallel paths.

risks.description: A local prefix is accidentally registered behind keyProbe and is permanently unavailable despite a running daemon.
risks.mitigation: Keyless descriptors occupy their own reachability-probed registry entry, and the required mutation proof demonstrates that keyProbe fails before the corrected probe succeeds.

risks.description: Five unreachable local defaults make sworn capabilities block serially or leak response content in errors.
risks.mitigation: Use one two-second timeout per request, launch probes concurrently, collect results deterministically by prefix, and expose only classified reasons without bodies.

risks.description: Capabilities/models use configured endpoints while the actual loop silently rebuilds config from environment and dispatches to defaults.
risks.mitigation: Create the provider config/registry once after config.Load and inject it into every production run path; command tests set a non-default fixture URL and fail if any request escapes it.

risks.description: The proof says local sworn run works but only a leaf client or fake driver was exercised.
risks.mitigation: The deterministic test starts at the sworn run command and observes real HTTP traffic from the implementer tool loop in a temporary git repo; proof additionally records an opt-in real local Ollama run or an explicit skip.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN sworn capabilities runs with configured keyless local endpoints THE SYSTEM SHALL concurrently GET each <base_url>/models with a two-second timeout, no retries, and no Authorization header; it SHALL render each prefix reachable only for HTTP 2xx plus a parseable data array and SHALL render classified unavailable reasons without bodies for every other result (C-01, C-03, N-02).

WHEN ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, or localai/ resolves for implementer, verifier, or captain THE SYSTEM SHALL return the keyless OAI in-process driver without consulting an API key, while ollama-cloud/ SHALL resolve through the credentialed OAI entry; the registered prefix sets SHALL be derived from S01 descriptors rather than a second hand-written list (N-02).

WHEN sworn models --provider <local-prefix> runs THE SYSTEM SHALL request the configured OAI /models endpoint, print returned model IDs under that same resolution prefix, and exit non-zero with a secret/payload-safe error when the transport or payload is invalid (C-01, C-03, N-02).

WHEN config.Load succeeds for capabilities, models, single-task run, release run, or parallel run THE SYSTEM SHALL construct one config-derived ProviderConfig/registry and inject it through startup resolution and every RunSlice call; no production command path SHALL rebuild a config-blind environment-only registry (C-01, N-02).

WHEN an end-to-end sworn run command is configured with ollama/<model> as implementer and capable fixture models as captain/verifier THE SYSTEM SHALL send multi-turn tool traffic to the configured local OAI endpoint, complete implement→verify→gated-merge with exit 0, and record that the local model served the implementer leg rather than a fallback (C-04, N-02).

WHEN the local availability guard is mutation-tested THE SYSTEM SHALL report the same local descriptor unavailable when temporarily wired to keyProbe with no key, then report it reachable after restoring the /models probe against a live HTTP transport; proof SHALL preserve both observed outputs (N-02).

IF a suitable local Ollama daemon/model is available during implementation THEN the implementer SHALL run the actual sworn run local-implementer journey and record command, output, and exit code; OTHERWISE proof SHALL name the daemon/model prerequisite that was unavailable and SHALL NOT substitute a unit test as live evidence (N-02).

THE SYSTEM SHALL keep all existing driver conformance, registry, catalog, run, and command tests green, and go test ./internal/driver/... ./internal/model/... ./internal/run/... ./cmd/sworn/... plus go vet ./... and gofmt -l SHALL pass (N-02).
